hook.text: Lava tubes are caves formed when the surface of a lava flow cools while molten rock drains away.

buildup.text: The hardened crust becomes a roof. The flowing lava beneath empties out, leaving a hollow tube.

discovery.text: Hawaii's Kazumura Cave is 65 km long—the world's longest lava tube.

twist.text: NASA studies lava tubes on the Moon and Mars as potential shelters for future astronauts.

climax.text: Lunar lava tubes could be kilometers wide—big enough to hold entire cities.

punchline.text: Earth's lava tubes may teach us how to live on the Moon.

quiz.question: How do lava tubes form?
options: The surface cools while molten lava drains out from inside
